own_review.issue: Fetch URL targets SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty/master/disclaimer.txt — the MAIN repo's master, where disclaimer.txt does not exist until this PR merges. Until then every CI/preview build renders the raw '404: Not Found' body inside the <pre> (no ok-check). Chicken-and-egg; the PR description's note about 'loading from my fork' is stale — the diff already points at the main repo.

No error handling: no response.ok check, no try/catch, no fallback UI. A GitHub raw outage throws during prerender (failed build/500); a 404 silently renders the error body as the disclaimer. Same flaw exists in privacy-policy/terms pages, but this is new code.

master-branch URL is mutable; the sibling privacy-policy page pins a commit SHA (c86ce857...). Better still: disclaimer.txt lives in the same repo — read it locally (fs/import) at build time and drop the network dependency entirely.
Next 15 prerenders this static route at build time, so the 'always fresh from GitHub' rationale is illusory — disclaimer.txt edits on master only appear after a redeploy.
Disclaimer text duplicated in README.md and disclaimer.txt; the two copies will drift.
Unrelated, undocumented styling change: ul flex-1 -> flex-auto and space-x-6 -> space-x-4 alters footer column balance site-wide (copyright and social columns remain flex-1).
Unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit 0.3.1 -> 0.3.4 bump (plus nested @eslint/core 0.15.1) with no package.json change — scope creep, though incidentally it picks up a dev-only ReDoS fix.

points.point: page.tsx:2-4 no error handling on fetch; cites terms/privacy-policy sharing the flaw and the client-side download page's try/catch + loading + Retry as the better in-repo pattern
points.note: Verified: terms/page.tsx and privacy-policy/page.tsx have no ok-check; download/page.tsx has try/catch (l.37-62), loading state (l.31), Retry button (l.102).

points.point: page.tsx:3 master URL mutable; privacy-policy pins commit c86ce857 — recommend pinning post-merge
points.note: Verified: privacy-policy fetches a commit-pinned raw URL.

points.point: README vs disclaimer.txt duplicated text, drift risk, suggests shared source or sync note
package-lock.json unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit 0.3.1->0.3.4 bump, should be split out
points.note: Verified: no package.json change in the PR.

points.point: Footer.tsx:16 flex-1->flex-auto + space-x-6->space-x-4 is an undocumented behavioral layout change; verify intent and narrow-screen layout
points.note: Verified against base Footer.tsx; correctly flags both the flex-grow and spacing change.

missed.finding: Fetch URL 404s until merge: main-repo master does not contain disclaimer.txt at base, so preview/CI builds render '404: Not Found' as page content (own_review #1)

exceeds: Pointing to the in-repo download page (try/catch, loading state, Retry button) as the concrete remediation pattern — a verified, sharper reference than the human review (which had no findings) and my own review.

points.point: page.tsx:4 claims the fetch URL 'points to the author's fork'
points.note: False: SaptarshiSarkar12/Drifty is the main repo (PR author is AvinashAbbigeri). v5 echoed the stale PR-description note instead of verifying the diff, which already contains the main-repo URL. Counted as an absence-fabrication (claims link not-updated when the real PR contains the updated URL).

points.point: page.tsx:4 recommend commit-pinned URL like privacy-policy (c86ce857); notes terms page also unpinned and fragile
points.note: Verified: privacy-policy pins a SHA; terms fetches master/LICENSE unpinned.

points.point: page.tsx:1-15 no error handling (no response.ok / try/catch / fallback); matches existing pages, suggests guard across all three legal pages
points.note: Verified. 'Will crash the build' is slightly overstated for a 404 (text() succeeds, error body rendered) but correct for network failures during prerender.

points.point: README vs disclaimer.txt duplication, drift risk, suggests linking /disclaimer instead
package-lock.json unrelated @eslint/plugin-kit bump, recommend reverting for scope

points.note: Raises no actionable concern and omits the flex-1->flex-auto behavioral half of the change that v4 caught.

qualitative: The human review is a bare approval with zero substantive findings, so both machine reviews trivially outperform it on this PR. v4, despite seeing only a truncated diff, produced the stronger review: every codebase reference checked out (privacy-policy's pinned SHA, terms' unpinned master fetch, the download page's try/catch+Retry pattern), and it correctly flagged the flex-1->flex-auto behavioral footer change. v5 covered nearly the same ground but committed one verified absence-fabrication: it asserted the fetch URL 'points to the author's fork' needing post-merge update, parroting the stale PR description when the actual diff already contains the main-repo URL. Neither candidate caught the sharpest issue — that main-repo master lacks disclaimer.txt until merge, so preview/CI builds render a raw '404: Not Found' as the disclaimer — though v5's URL point at least lands on the same remediation. Net: v4 +4, v5 +3; v4 wins on factual discipline.
